search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG157 joint replacement postoperative rehabilitation
candidate_sources_rejected: VTE guideline as complete postoperative coverage
rejection_reasons: The workflow spans procedures and cannot inherit one operation-specific schedule.
VTE guidance covers one risk domain only.
Patient pages are not exact clinician guidance.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for postoperative wound care across surgery and rehabilitation after primary elective hip, knee, or shoulder replacement; not exact for every orthopaedic operation.
setting_applicability: Partial: UK perioperative, community, and joint-replacement services versus UAE outpatient follow-up.
UAE_applicability: No direct UAE applicability is established; UAE sources were searched and the NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG125 and NG157, last reviewed in 2023 and 2024 respectively, were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: NG125 and NG157 remain current official guidance.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped generic postoperative and wound-care documentation fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped wound redness, discharge, swelling, pain, fever, microbiology, and concern fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped pain, recovery, return-to-work or activity, wound, question, and information fields for primary joint replacement.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documentation of postoperative pain, wound care, recovery, rehabilitation, return to work or activity, concerns, questions, and procedure-specific information.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped mobility, function, rehabilitation, and physiotherapy fields for primary joint replacement.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented mobility, activities of daily living, rehabilitation goals, self-directed or supervised rehabilitation, functional impairment, and physiotherapy or occupational-therapy follow-up.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped follow-up for new or worsening pain, limp, or loss of function after joint replacement.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports orthopaedic review for new or worsening pain, limp, or loss of function related to a joint replacement while noting the absence of a general fixed monitoring schedule.

unresolved_source_gaps: Calf pain, shortness of breath, DVT prophylaxis, neurovascular checks, operative-note review, generic postoperative imaging, fixed suture-removal or six-week schedules, and procedure-specific restrictions remain unsupported.
Post-surgical diagnosis remains clinician-stated and procedure specific.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
